Scenario.title: Operation Ghost: Targeting Transportation Systems
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting transportation infrastructure, leveraging OSINT, custom tools, and advanced persistence techniques to compromise systems and cause operational disruption.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of critical infrastructure for geopolitical leverage

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Public Information Source, Script, Internet-facing assets, OS Process, Process, Windows Service, Domain Name Registrar, File, Public IP/Network Block
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Open-Source Intelligence (OSINT) Gathering, Script Execution (Mechanism), General Access Method, Code Injection, OS API Execution (Mechanism), Exploitation for Initial Access, Process Hollowing, Process Access (Mechanism), Network Service Scanning, Pass the Hash, File Access (Mechanism), Process Creation (Mechanism), File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers gather publicly available data about transportation systems, including vendor details and network configurations, to identify potential vulnerabilities in SCADA/ICS environments.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: Specialized scripts are developed to exploit protocol weaknesses in transportation logistics systems, enabling covert command-and-control communication.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Spear-phishing emails with malicious attachments are sent to insiders, tricking them into executing payloads that establish initial footholds in the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Malware is injected into legitimate processes to bypass endpoint detection mechanisms and execute arbitrary code on compromised systems.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Attackers manipulate access tokens to maintain persistent access across reboots, ensuring continued control over compromised systems.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-679: Exploitation of Improperly Configured or Implemented Memory Protections
stage.description: Vulnerabilities in memory protections are exploited to escalate privileges and access restricted administrative resources.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-671: Requirements for ASIC Functionality Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Malicious code is hidden within legitimate processes by replacing their memory contents, evading detection by security software.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-679: Exploitation of Improperly Configured or Implemented Memory Protections
stage.description: Credentials are extracted from memory using LSASS manipulation to enable lateral movement across the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network scans identify critical assets and pathways, allowing attackers to prioritize targets for further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used to move laterally across the network, compromising additional systems and expanding the attack surface.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Operational data is collected from local files to prepare for exfiltration and subsequent disruption of transportation systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is stealthily exfiltrated using process creation techniques disguised as legitimate network traffic.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Malicious code corrupts critical files, causing operational disruptions and damaging transportation infrastructure.

Scenario.summary: Operation Ghost employs a complex, multi-phase attack to infiltrate and disrupt transportation systems, combining social engineering, custom tooling, and advanced persistence techniques to achieve sustained access and maximize impact.
